channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Research Highlights Watermelon’s Nutritional and Heart Health Benefits
item.description: Recent research indicates that watermelon is more than just a hydrating summer fruit—it may play a significant role in improving overall diet quality and supporting cardiovascular health. Studies usin...

item.title: Optimal Greening launches sustainable water project in Ikorodu North communities
item.description: Optimal Greening don officially start Project SWIFT, wey be sustainable water initiative, for Isiwu and Eginrin Ekun communities for Ikorodu North, Lagos. Dis project dey aim to give people clean wate...

item.title: Gauteng Schools Struggle Under R600 Million Municipal Debt, Threatening Services
item.description: Gauteng's education system is under severe pressure as schools face a municipal debt approaching R600 million. The crisis is disrupting electricity and water supply, with hundreds of schools at risk o...

item.title: CBSE mandates study of three languages for Class 9, including two Indian languages
item.description: The Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE) has announced that from 1st July, students of Class 9 will be required to study three languages as part of their curriculum. According to the circular i...

item.title: Anthony Scaramucci describes Donald Trump's hidden psychological tendencies
item.description: Anthony Scaramucci, former White House communications director and now outspoken critic of Donald Trump, has provided insight into what he considers a key psychological trait of the former president. ...

item.title: Advocates urge inclusion of electronics permits in National Building Code review to improve broadband rollout
item.description: CitizenWatch Philippines has called for electronics permit requirements to be included in the ongoing reassessment of the National Building Code. The group highlighted that outdated and inconsistent l...

item.title: Scott Hastings, Scottish Rugby Legend and Broadcaster, Passes Away at 61
item.description: Scott Hastings, one of Scotland's most celebrated rugby union players, has died aged 61. Hastings, who played alongside his brother Gavin, was part of Scotland's last Grand Slam-winning team in the 19...

item.title: Court Awards Former Sales Executive KSh 4.87 Million for Harassment, Defamation, and Unfair Termination
item.description: A Nairobi court has awarded KSh 4,876,800 to Muthoni Andabwa, a former sales and marketing executive at Arichem Ltd, after ruling she faced harassment, defamation, and unfair dismissal. Muthoni worked...

item.title: Police yet to arrest suspect in DHA Karachi fatal hit-and-run accident
item.description: KARACHI: Three days after a tragic hit-and-run incident on Sea View Road near Bukhari Commercial in DHA Phase VI, the suspect driver is still at large despite police recovering the involved vehicle. T...

item.title: Review and analysis of the 2026 International Booker Prize shortlist
item.description: This year’s International Booker Prize shortlist features a diverse selection of translated fiction from around the world, showcasing both geographical variety and stylistic range. The contenders incl...

item.title: Blue Jays Pitcher Jose Berrios to Undergo Elbow Surgery, Season in Doubt
item.description: Toronto Blue Jays pitcher Jose Berrios is scheduled for elbow surgery to address a stress fracture in his right elbow. The procedure, set to be performed by Dr. Keith Meister, may be more complex if l...

item.title: Bengaluru police register case after man abuses cow
item.description: In Bengaluru, the city police have registered a legal case following the circulation of a video on social media that allegedly showed a man sexually assaulting a cow in the Akkipete area. The video, p...

item.title: NAFDAC Confirms BON Bread Meets All Safety and Regulatory Requirements After Investigation
item.description: The National Agency for Food and Drug Administration and Control (NAFDAC) don carry out proper investigation on the matter wey people dey talk about BON Bread wey some person claim say e no dey get mo...

item.title: Study Suggests Infrasound May Cause 'Haunted' Feelings in Homes
item.description: A team of Canadian scientists, led by Dr. Rodney Schmaltz of MacEwan University in Edmonton, has investigated why some people feel uneasy in old or supposedly haunted buildings. Their research points ...

item.title: Dr. Hook frontman Dennis Locorriere passes away at 76 following long kidney disease battle
item.description: Dennis Locorriere, the frontman of the 1970s soft rock band Dr. Hook, has died at the age of 76 after a long and courageous battle with kidney disease. He passed away peacefully on 16th May 2026, surr...

item.title: Trump's credibility issues could influence 2026 midterm elections, attorney says
item.description: Attorney and columnist Alan Scott Bolden has highlighted potential political consequences stemming from President Donald Trump's credibility issues. Bolden argues that Trump's history of false or misl...

item.title: OPP charges several Muskoka drivers in crackdown on impaired and dangerous driving
item.description: Ontario Provincial Police (OPP) recently conducted a focused enforcement operation in the Muskoka region targeting impaired and dangerous driving. During this blitz, multiple drivers were arrested and...

item.title: Perth surveyor Steven Mattaboni dies in shark attack at Rottnest Island
item.description: Steven Mattaboni, a 38-year-old surveyor from Perth, tragically lost his life in a shark attack at Horseshoe Reef near Rottnest Island on Saturday morning. An experienced fisherman and devoted father,...

item.title: Javier Bardem claims Hollywood is shifting away from blacklisting actors who support Palestine
item.description: Oscar-winning actor Javier Bardem has stated that Hollywood is undergoing a significant change regarding actors who publicly support Palestine. Speaking at the Cannes Film Festival while promoting his...

item.title: Motorway Authorities Restrict Vehicles Carrying Sacrificial Animals Ahead of Eidul Azha for Safety
item.description: Islamabad ki National Highway and Motorway Police (NHMP) nay announcement kiya hai ke Eidul Azha se pehlay, motorways par janwar le jaane wali gaadiyon ka entry banned hai. Official statement ke mutab...
